meta.book: Lay, Linear Algebra and Its Applications

items.label: 1a
items.text: If A is a 2×2 matrix with a zero determinant, then one column of A is a multiple of the other.

items.label: 1b
items.text: If two rows of a 3×3 matrix A are the same, then det A = 0.

items.label: 1c
items.text: If A is a 3×3 matrix, then det 5A = 5 det A.

items.label: 1d
items.text: If A and B are n×n matrices, with det A = 2 and det B = 3, then det(A + B) = 5.

items.label: 1e
items.text: If A is n×n and det A = 2, then det A^3 = 6.

items.label: 1f
items.text: If B is produced by interchanging two rows of A, then det B = det A.

items.label: 1g
items.text: If B is produced by multiplying row 3 of A by 5, then det B = 5 · det A.

items.label: 1h
items.text: If B is formed by adding to one row of A a linear combination of the other rows, then det B = det A.

items.label: 1l
items.text: Any system of n linear equations in n variables can be solved by Cramer’s rule.

items.label: 1m
items.text: If u and v are in R^2 and det [u  v] = 10, then the area of the triangle in the plane with vertices at 0, u, and v is 10.

items.label: 1o
items.text: If A is invertible, then det A^−1 = det A.

items.label: 1p
items.text: If A is invertible, then (det A)(det A^−1) = 1.
